speaker: AGRIPPA
text: What, are the brothers parted?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: They have dispatched with Pompey; he is gone.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The other three are sealing. Octavia weeps

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To part from Rome. Caesar is sad, and Lepidus,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Since Pompey’s feast, as Menas says, is troubled

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: With the greensickness.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: ’Tis a noble Lepidus.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A very fine one. O, how he loves Caesar!

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Nay, but how dearly he adores Mark Antony!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Caesar? Why, he’s the Jupiter of men.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: What’s Antony? The god of Jupiter.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Spake you of Caesar? How, the nonpareil!

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: O Antony, O thou Arabian bird!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Would you praise Caesar, say “Caesar.” Go no further.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Indeed, he plied them both with excellent praises.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But he loves Caesar best, yet he loves Antony.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Hoo, hearts, tongues, figures, scribes, bards, poets, cannot

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Think, speak, cast, write, sing, number—hoo!—

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: His love to Antony. But as for Caesar,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Kneel down, kneel down, and wonder.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Both he loves.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: They are his shards and he their beetle.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: So,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: This is to horse. Adieu, noble Agrippa.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Good fortune, worthy soldier, and farewell.

speaker: ANTONY
text: No further, sir.

speaker: CAESAR
text: You take from me a great part of myself.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Use me well in ’t.—Sister, prove such a wife

speaker: CAESAR
text: As my thoughts make thee, and as my farthest bond

speaker: CAESAR
text: Shall pass on thy approof.—Most noble Antony,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Let not the piece of virtue which is set

speaker: CAESAR
text: Betwixt us, as the cement of our love

speaker: CAESAR
text: To keep it builded, be the ram to batter

speaker: CAESAR
text: The fortress of it. For better might we

speaker: CAESAR
text: Have loved without this mean, if on both parts

speaker: CAESAR
text: This be not cherished.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Make me not offended

speaker: ANTONY
text: In your distrust.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have said.

speaker: ANTONY
text: You shall not find,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Though you be therein curious, the least cause

speaker: ANTONY
text: For what you seem to fear. So the gods keep you,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And make the hearts of Romans serve your ends.

speaker: ANTONY
text: We will here part.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Farewell, my dearest sister, fare thee well.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The elements be kind to thee and make

speaker: CAESAR
text: Thy spirits all of comfort. Fare thee well.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: My noble brother.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The April’s in her eyes. It is love’s spring,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And these the showers to bring it on.—Be cheerful.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Sir, look well to my husband’s house, and—

speaker: CAESAR
text: What, Octavia?

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: I’ll tell you in your ear.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Her tongue will not obey her heart, nor can

speaker: ANTONY
text: Her heart inform her tongue—the swan’s-down feather

speaker: ANTONY
text: That stands upon the swell at the full of tide

speaker: ANTONY
text: And neither way inclines.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Will Caesar weep?

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: He has a cloud in ’s face.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: He were the worse for that were he a horse;

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: So is he being a man.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Why, Enobarbus,

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: When Antony found Julius Caesar dead,

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: He cried almost to roaring. And he wept

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: When at Philippi he found Brutus slain.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: That year indeed he was troubled with a rheum.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: What willingly he did confound he wailed,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Believe ’t, till I wept too.

speaker: CAESAR
text: No, sweet Octavia,

speaker: CAESAR
text: You shall hear from me still. The time shall not

speaker: CAESAR
text: Outgo my thinking on you.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Come, sir, come,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I’ll wrestle with you in my strength of love.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Look, here I have you, thus I let you go,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And give you to the gods.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Adieu, be happy.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Let all the number of the stars give light

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: To thy fair way.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Farewell, farewell.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Farewell.
